title: Processing of red deer samples for Genotyping
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"iaef","text":"The threat of isolation to red deer (Cervus elaphus) has been described in numerous European studies. The consequences range from reduced genetic diversity and increased inbreeding to inbreeding depression. It has been shown that the underlying factors cannot be generalised, but vary greatly in their effects depending on local conditions. The aim of this study was to analyse in detail the genetics of red deer in a large German federal state with a population density of 532 inhabitants per km2 and 23.8% settlement and traffic area, in order to generate data for future management of the region. 1199 individual samples of red deer were collected in all 20 Administrative Management Units (AMUs) and compared with existing results from the\nneighbouring state of Hesse (19 AMUs). All 2490 individuals from both states were clustered using Bayesian methods and connectivity between neighbouring AMUs was quantified. Overall, 30% of the AMUs were found to be highly isolated, mostly with effective population sizes (Ne) \u003c 100. In contrast, 47.5% of the AMUs still had clear connectivity, allowing them to be merged into 4 larger red deer regions. For the small isolated areas, low genetic diversity was found in unit with high homozygosity and low Ne. With high sampling density and\nidentical methodology, detailed information on AMUs can be obtained and the degree of vulnerability of individual AMUs as part of the overall population can specifically be validated. Such data can help improve future wildlife management.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":37,"length":14},{"style":"sup","offset":496,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"6djsp","text":"All the necessary information can be found in the publication (to be provided as soon as it is published). This is where you will find all the additional details that may help to put this information into practice.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"eea3l","text":"Before start make sure that you have a good sample, including ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a56mh","text":"- enough animals per population (we recommend 60)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"79oji","text":"- enough populations","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2vveq","text":"- a good mixture of individuals from the respective population","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: DNA extraction from tissue samples withInstant Virus RNA Kit delta PREP

Preparation of the Virus RNA Kit for first use (Label kit with expiry date) MixWashing Solution HS with 70 ml 96-99.8 % ethanol (from the chemical cabinetbelow the fume cupboard) MixWashing Solution LS with 144 ml 96-99.8 % ethanol Checkoff on the container in each case

Preparation for extraction with 24 samplesà while incubating samples on shaker! 24x Extraction Cups, 72x Receiver Tubes, 24x purple Spin Filters, 24x Elution Tubes for extracted DNA Label all lids with sample numbers

DNA extraction Select max. 24 samples with tissue (in 2 ml epis) (more will not fit in the centrifuge) + 500 μl Lysis Buffer RL each (for bone chips 800 - 1000 μl Lysis Buffer RL+ 20 μl Proteinase K) + one grinding ball each Load ball mill evenly (CAVE: close both caps, also pay attention to unused side, if < 24 samples insert empty epis in the corners) and run at 25 Hertz/s for 2 min Remove grinding balls with bar magnets (with glove over) and clean in DNA-Ex Incubation on the shaker (1000/min) for 30 - 60 min at RT (the longer, the more effective); (for bone chips, incubate for at least 10 h) Centrifuge for 1 min at 13,000 rpm (for bone chips centrifuge for 3 min) Pipette off 400 μl of the supernatant and transfer to a new 2ml extraction cup (IMPORTANT: pipette carefully so as not to pick up the pellet, otherwise the Spin Filter membrane will clog later! + 400 μl Binding Solution RBS (if more supernatant was pipetted off for bone chips, also adjust the RBS volumeà 1:1 (μl supernatant : μl RBS) Pre-text 10 s (important!) Pipette 650 μl RL-RBS sample into the prepared Spin Filter plus Receiver Tube Centrifuge for 1 min at 13,000 rpm (if the solution does not completely pass through the SpinFilter, then centrifuge again, if necessary clean the SpinFilter very carefully with a pipette tip) Place Spin Filter on a new Receiver Tube, discard used Receiver Tube (liquid in organic waste) + 500 μl Washing Solution HS Centrifuge for 1 min at 13,000 rpm (if the solution does not completely pass through the SpinFilter, centrifuge again) Set Spin Filter to a new Receiver Tube, discard used Receiver Tube + 500 μl Washing Solution LS Centrifuge for 3 min at 13,000 rpm (if the solution does not completely pass through the SpinFilter, centrifuge again) Place the Spin Filter in a new 1.5 ml Elution Cup, discard the used Receiver Tube + 50 μl RNAse-free water (for tissue with a very high DNA concentration such as lung or liver, use 60 μl RNAse-free water for dilution; for muscle samples possibly only 40 μl) Incubate for 3 min at room temperature Centrifuge for 1 min at 8,000 rpm Discard spin filter; DNA is now in 1.5 ml cup

Qubit Flex dsDNA Br Kit (Broad Range) or dsDNA Hs Kit (High Sensitivity, with less DNA per sample) Contains: Buffer Colorant Standard #1 and Standard #2 o Always store in the refrigerator Ingredients are sensitive to lightAlways keep in a dark place and store in a cupboard Total volume in one container always 200μl

Preparation of the DNA measurement: Standard measurement required? Every +/- 100 samples new measurement with the respective standard (see list atQubit)à if yes, see below Light off Always take the kit out of the cupboard for as short a time as possible and work quickly Gloves on Prepare number of multistrips Mix DNA on vortex, if thawed or stood for a longer period of time Pipette 3 μl per DNA into multistrips Reagent calculator I. Number of samples II. Standards III. Include surplus: e.g. 2 extra samples For <10 samples, simply calculate one more sample As a reserve for pipetting Mixing the buffer-dye mixture I. Pour the buffer into a small glass beaker II. Add colorant III. Mix with pipette and swirl Pipette 197 μl of the buffer-dye mixture to the DNA in each case Lid closed Incubate for 2 minutes (if preparation has not already taken a long time) Testing: dsDNA Br/ dsDNA Set sample range: Setting Standard measurement and sample measurement: when a standard is running Without standards: Sample measurement Deselect unfilled tubes by on them Select units ng/μl Measureà CAVE: There must be no air bubbles! Core area: this is where the results should lie Extended range: the values are only targeted here o If sample is too high then dilute o If the sample is too small, nothing can be done and it may still be sufficient for capillary electrophoresis (possibly use 2 μl instead of 1 μl DNA for PCR if < 1 ng/μl) (Data can be found again under Data (Home screen)) Enter the number of samples measured in the table (depending on the buffer) to knowwhen the next standard measurement is required (add to the samples already measured) Enter results in Excel red deer file

Standard measurement Every +/- 100 samples: new measurement with the respective standard Always use the appropriate buffer and dye for the standard! Solutions must be at roomtemperature 10 μl each of standard solution #1 in eight tubes (1 multistipe) and 10 μl each of standard solution #2 in eight tubes Add 190 μl buffer dye mixture to each Measure: Main menu again dsDNA Br/ dsDNAHs -> Read standards and measure samples Enter standard measurement in list

Copy the corresponding sample numbers in the Red Deer Excel table and paste the values on the "PCR assays" page twice for each PCR number (once for MM1 and once for MM2) Leave the first field blank for the PCR standards (BKW_059) Enter measured DNA content Automatic calculation of the required dilution (target: 2.5 ng/μl)à CAVE: possibly set to a target of 5ng/μl Label 0.5ml cup Mix DNA in vortex Add the calculated amount of RNAse-free water (from DNA extraction kit) to Eppis; if there is a number in the column next to H 2 O (>5:250) (if there is > 250 μl H 2 O to be added), then first dilute 5:45 (= 1/10) and then the number next to it (5:x); this saves RNAse-free water. Pipette 5 μl of the respective DNA into the tubes (V(DNA) = 5 μlà if less, pipetting error is too high).

Check whether there is enough BKW59 standard (in the freezer of the capillary sequencer room) or whether it also has to go into the PCR First of all: Place the master mixes in the refrigerator in the "Sample addition" room todefrost! A maximum of 24 samples can be processed per PCR device (23 samples are possible in the capillarysequencer) Place the rack with the DNA in the "PCR preparation" room in the refrigerator. Space "Sample addition"à No DNA here! In the freezer, usually on the top shelf, there is a box labeled "new MS Hirsch (for Kap. Sequencer)" Check the volume of the mastermixes or primer mixes (mastermixes: MM H1 and MM H2). Is there enough primer left?à Draw up the required amount with a pipette and check whether enough is drawn up a. If not, then prepare new mastermixes from the individual forward and reverseprimers: Instructions in Oligo folder under "Capillary sequencingdeer" (mix primer mixes by snipping, then piepette primer mixes with waterand mix 2xMM b. If you have Eppendorf tubes labeled MM H1 and MM H2, take them out and put them inthe refrigerator to thaw, so do this first! Label 0.2 ml single tubes in PCR tray without lid 2x 23 (23 per master mix; e.g. first 23 sample numbers in black, second 23 sample numbers in green Pivot MasterMix 9μl MM1 in the first half (1-23) of the 9μl MM2 into the other half (24-46) of the tubes Put the master mixes back in the freezer Change room to "PCR preparation" room (take PCR trays with 46 tubes) Now switch on the PCR device for preheating, as it takes approx. 20 minà see below Mix the DNA sample (flick it and then shake it down) 1μl DNA in each tube (if < 1μl DNA is measured, you can also use 2 μl) with shrink-wrapped filter pipette tips; 2x each, as two master mixes Mix the DNA with the master mix: Place lid on to mix and invert until liquid collects in the lid, then knock back down into the tip %s %s Room change Switch on the PCR device, if not alreadydone (always takes a while to heat up): Start/Stopà Select the program from the list andenter the number (HermannàProgram 20 (HIRSCH 60))àEnter (program is now running); Only preheat and do not start PCR yet: additionallystart/stop afterwardsàPause Adjust samples (if full, remove emptytubes, otherwise leave in the corners), close and screw shut If preheated: Start/Stopà "Continue"; then the programruns (approx. 2h) Get new PCR sample racks with lids andlabel them for later freezing Check whether the correct program isrunning, "Info" shows the duration it still WhenPCR ready: Flashes "Pause" and 4° Open the lid and take out the samples Switch off the device

Denaturation Remove the formamide with O standard from the freezer and allow to thaw in the dark (protect from light); (if no formamide with O standard is ready mixed, then mix the prepared 1200 μl formamidewith 15 μl O standard) Preheat the water bath to 95°C Sort 48 x 0.5 μl Eppi into rack (CAVE: where is 1?) and label; standard is Eppi 1 and 25 Place 12μl formamide in each Eppi Add 1 μl BKW059 standard each to Eppi 1 and Eppi 25 Add 1μl of the respective PCR DNA samples to the remaining Eppis and then place on a new small blue rack, always leaving a hole free (6 tubes per row) Place the rack with tweezers in the water bath at 95° for 3 minutes so that the rack floats Briefly drain the rack and immediately place in the freezer for 2 minutes Switch off the water bath Formamide back in the freezer Sequencing or freezing samples (small blue rack in purple box)

Capillary sequencing CAVE: first test/rinse run if capillary sequencer has not been run for several days (runany sample) Start up PC Open the sequencer and remove the purple box! Switching on the sequencer Start ABI Prism310 program on PC (Option to save 20 minutes: Preheat temperature to 60°C: Windowsà Manuel controlà Temperature setà 60à Execute Create sample sheet a. New Genscan Sample sheet 48 b. Set 5 dyes at the top right (default with "diamond" must be orange) c. Enter sample name d. Close Save As Naming the injection list Set samples a. Open sequencer b. Press "Tray" c. Sorting samples CAVE: A1 rear right d. Retract tray with the same button IMPORTANT: Check whether there is enough gel in the glass syringe (> 0.2 ml), if too little, see below IMPORTANT: Fill buffer in container 1 up to the black line; fill Roth water in container 2 up to the black line; fill Roth water up to the edge of Eppi in position 3 Start run a. New Genscan Injection List b. sample sheet just created c. Insert "Laser off" last in the open file for KS2!!! (not necessary for KS1) Moduleà Laser off.md5 i. If 48 samples then: Edità Add (inserts new line) ii. Set moduleà Laser off md.5 iii. And give the sample a name (any name) d. RUN (takes approx. 19 hours) If gel < 0.2, then it must be topped up: Pop7Aliqu. Remove the buffer from the top freezer compartment and defrost atapprox. 50° in an ultrasonic bath In program ABI Prism310: Window Manual Controlà Syringe Home (moves up) Shift syringe drive toggle to the left Unscrew the gel syringe and fill it bubble-free with Pop7 Aliqu. Buffer up to0.7-0.8ml Screw the gel syringe back in; turn the waste valve carefully and, if necessary, pressthe glass syringe down slightly until all the air bubbles run to the left ordown over the "crossing"; then tighten again Move syringe drive toggle to the right On the computer: Syringe down in careful steps until the syringe drive is lightlyapplied

Sampling and data collection When Run is finished, close the Run window/injection list and save it Close program Drag Run in Explorer onto the USB stick Shut down PC Move tray out and remove samples; provide samples with labeled lids and freeze in bags Move tray Push the purple box under the autosampler! Switch off the device
creator: Gerald Reiner
